content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4

## Summary

Reject the amendment. Its core premise is incorrect: deserializing into `serde_json::Value` does not make this impl JSON-only. It can still work with other Serde formats such as TOML or YAML when the input maps cleanly onto the JSON value model, so documenting the type as "JSON-only" would be misleading.

## Votes

- `fr-8-93dc626c`: Reject
  `serde_json::Value::deserialize(deserializer)` is format-agnostic at the Serde layer; it accepts any deserializer that can produce a JSON-compatible data model. Likewise, `serde_json::from_value` only consumes the already-built `Value` tree and does not care whether that tree originated from JSON, TOML, YAML, or another Serde source. The real limitation is not "JSON-only" but "JSON-value-shaped input only," which is inherent to choosing `serde_json::Value` as the intermediate representation. Because the amendment claims non-JSON formats will fail at runtime as a general rule, it does not identify a real defect, and adding a doc comment asserting JSON-only behavior would misstate the implementation.
